text: Derived (mechanistic) prediction for q-0011, sharpening a-0014 (H-MG): because the externalization saving = the no-tools agent's own in-head reasoning-token volume, and the Goldilocks peak sits where that volume is largest, the peak must track each model's in-head reasoning frontier. A weaker model's in-head blow-up occurs at LOWER search_nodes, so its peak of [out_saved*P_OUT - cr_added*P_CR] shifts to an easier rung. MG1' (falsifiable): peak of that per-trial usage estimator over the difficulty ladder is non-decreasing in model capability (Haiku <= Sonnet <= Opus). This is computable directly from the planned e-0010 cross-model usage records, so the test is pre-specified with a concrete estimator rather than a phenomenological cost ratio. Status inconclusive: no cross-model data exists yet (the e-0010 sweep needs a paid window); this is a prediction grounded in the verified single-model mechanism, not a result.
